text: Which of the following is a quick-commerce platform?
explanation: Blinkit, Zepto and Swiggy Instamart are all quick-commerce (10-minute grocery delivery) platforms.

text: Which of the following is NOT a Japanese manufacturer?
explanation: Nissan, Toyota and Honda are Japanese; Skoda is a Czech automobile manufacturer (part of the Volkswagen Group).

text: Who is credited with the Just-In-Time (JIT) manufacturing philosophy?
explanation: The Just-In-Time production system was pioneered by Toyota as part of the Toyota Production System.

text: In which Indian state is the hill station Pachmarhi located?
explanation: Pachmarhi, the 'Queen of the Satpura', is in Madhya Pradesh.

text: In which Indian state does the river Damodar originate?
explanation: The Damodar rises in the Chota Nagpur plateau of Jharkhand and flows into West Bengal.

text: Who is the current Managing Director / CEO of Sun Pharmaceutical Industries?
explanation: Kirti Ganorkar took over as Managing Director of Sun Pharma in 2025, with founder Dilip Shanghvi becoming Executive Chairman.

text: In which part of the world is the region of Patagonia located?
explanation: Patagonia spans the southern parts of Argentina and Chile in South America.

text: In which country is Nvidia headquartered?
explanation: Nvidia is headquartered in Santa Clara, California, USA.

text: What is the approximate length of India's Golden Quadrilateral highway network?
explanation: The Golden Quadrilateral connecting Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai and Kolkata is about 5,846 km long.

text: Who became Nepal's Prime Minister in March 2026?
explanation: Balen Shah became Nepal's Prime Minister in March 2026.

text: What was India's rank in the 2025 Human Development Index (HDI)?
explanation: India was ranked 130th in the Human Development Index released in the 2025 Human Development Report.

text: In which Indian state is Hindon airport located?
explanation: Hindon Airport is located in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: It will be hard to ______ John now that you have so incensed him.
explanation: John is already incensed (angry), so it will be hard to mollify (pacify/soothe) him. The other words mean to harm or to anger further.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: In a fit of ______ she threw out the valuable statue simply because it had belonged to her ex-husband.
explanation: A 'fit of pique' is a sudden burst of irritation or resentment, which fits an impulsive, spiteful act.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: For centuries there was no peace between their descendants; in fact ______ strife continued until modern times.
explanation: 'Internecine' strife is mutually destructive conflict between members of the same group, matching feuding descendants.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: Our once thriving College Animal Club is now ______; the programs have had to be cancelled due to lack of support.
explanation: With programs cancelled and no support, the club is now 'defunct' (no longer active or in existence).

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: Moths are nocturnal pollinators, visiting scented flowers during the hours of darkness, whereas butterflies are ______, attracted to bright flowers in the daytime.
explanation: As the opposite of nocturnal, butterflies are 'diurnal' (active during the daytime).

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: We are so caught up in the myths of the best and the brightest and the self-made that we think ______ spring naturally from the earth.
explanation: The sentence echoes Malcolm Gladwell's 'Outliers'; we wrongly think 'outliers' (exceptional people) arise on their own.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: Gaurav and Ishita plan to get married in summer, but Ishita's family ______ it to happen in winter.
explanation: 'Family' is treated as a singular unit here and the tense parallels 'plan', so 'wants' is correct.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: He was formal and polite, but he struck us as condescending, ______ them with his mathematical certitude.
explanation: A condescending manner would annoy people, so 'infuriating' them fits the negative tone.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: There is one world in which I believe the habit of mistaking luck for skill is most prevalent - and most conspicuous - and ______ is the world of markets.
explanation: '...and that is the world of markets' correctly points back to the world just described.

text: Fill in the blank with the most suitable word: The dominant primordial beast was strong in Buck, and under the ______ conditions of trail life it grew and grew.
explanation: The harsh sled-trail life in 'The Call of the Wild' is best described as 'fierce', which would strengthen the primal instinct.
